name: Nebruz

location.description: Western bank of the river through the swamp; accessible via the Caelrun swamp path.

population.character: Fugitives, escaped slaves, failed merchants, desperate adventurers. Gruff, closed-mouthed, fiercely communal when threatened. Outsiders called 'dryboots.'
population.demographics: Predominantly human. Heavily male (~4:1). Fewer than a dozen children. Mature adults; young leave, old do not survive.

physical.construction: Wood pilings over mud; a few buildings on dry hummocks. Brick for sturdier structures. Reed-thatched roofs. Boardwalks average 3 ft above ground; rush brands and coal braziers after dark.
physical.atmosphere: Grey-green moss on blackened timbers. Witch's beard in the trees. Tea-brown water. Frogs, and screams from the swamp after nightfall.

marsh_hand.name: Phaid Torngrasp
marsh_hand.title: Marsh-Hand
marsh_hand.role: Combined mayor and magistrate. Elected by popular support. Holds court at his residence; no formal village hall.

sentinel.title: Sentinel
sentinel.role: Sheriff and captain of watch. Commands 10 enforcers.

enforcers.equipment: Ring mail, cudgels, short swords, manacles.

government.law: Swift justice; no prolonged trials or incarceration. Dryboots structurally disadvantaged before the Marsh-Hand.

economy.secondary: Smuggling hub; private criminal and political dealings.
economy.notable: Honey and mead trade with the froggish-folk. Exotic herb market for alchemists and sorcerers.

defenses.natural: Swamp terrain on landward side. Quicksand fields ring the village.
defenses.constructed: 12 watchtowers on 20-ft posts in a semi-circle. Spiked pits and deadfalls in the swamp approaches.
defenses.murkwatch: Every able-bodied adult serves 2 shifts/month by lottery. Failure is a fineable offense.
defenses.militia: Armed populace; veteran warriors and a few magic-users. Guerilla tactics if overwhelmed.

allies.basis: inherently unthreatening and governed by lizard-men; active mead and honeycomb trade

associated_npcs.name: Leeandra
associated_npcs.role: Swamp priestess; lizard-man diplomatic keystone for the Luric marches
associated_npcs.location: Swamp, several hours southwest of Nebruz

locations.name: Nebruz Harbor
locations.description: Muddy riverbank ringed by wooden quays. Shallow from silt buildup; large boats moor at pilings and send cargo ashore by jollyboat.
locations.services: Docking fees: 1 sp/day (skiff), 5 sp per 10 ft of length (larger vessels).

locations.name: The Painted Frog
locations.description: Three-story inn. Crude frog statue at the door. Curtained booths frequently used for clandestine meetings. Ship's bell by the bar summons the enforcers from across the street.
locations.services: Common room 5 cp/night; private room 1 gp/night. Meals 2 cp–1 gp. Drinks 1 cp–2 gp. Specialty: mushroom-stuffed eel on wild rice.

locations.name: Mother's Boarding House
locations.description: Hulking three-story brick building for transient laborers and those who cannot afford the inn. Does not rent to adventurers.
locations.services: 5 sp/week: cot, blanket, footlocker, two meals of gruel and hard bread daily.

locations.name: Enforcers' Post
locations.description: Squat brick building. One enforcer always on duty; one to two on patrol. Armory: 2 spare ring mail sets, cudgels, 4 short swords. Small holding cell. Iron gong outside summons all enforcers.

locations.name: Alligator Pit
locations.description: 30x20 ft water pit enclosed by a 4-ft wooden fence. Home to four female alligators (~11 ft each). Death sentences carried out by plank-walk at spear-point.
locations.services: Public feedings twice daily; 1 sp to observe.

npc.name: Gerum o' the Spike
npc.role: Enforcer and alligator warden; lost his left hand, replaced with a metal hook

locations.name: Swamp Rat Provisions
locations.description: Cluttered general store. No posted prices — all purchases require haggling. Stocks nails, jerky, arrows, knives, wood-axes. Locked warehouse outbuilding (6a).

locations.name: The Bloated Leech
locations.description: Low-roofed, open-plan interior. Plank-and-barrel bar. Poor illumination. Specialty: 'bog water,' a fiery liquor fermented from swamp tubers; dead leech in each bottle, said to bring good fortune if swallowed.
locations.services: Dreg beer 1 cp; bog water 2 gp/bottle.

npc.name: Adlaba Kurn
npc.role: Proprietor; eldest son Gerly (F2) serves as bouncer

locations.name: Punishment Post
locations.description: Eight-foot denuded tree trunk in an earthen plaza. Site of corporal punishment; doubles as village green. Avoided after dark — believed haunted by the spirits of those executed here.

locations.name: Warehouses
locations.description: Two large two-story buildings. River traders rent space for goods in transit. Single underpaid keeper per warehouse — susceptible to bribes. Regularly exploited by smugglers.

locations.name: Fish House
locations.description: Buys fresh catches daily; salts, smokes, and packs fish for distant markets in the rear. Pays well for exotic catches: giant crayfish, swamp eels, giant crabs.

locations.name: Meat & Skins
locations.description: Combined butchery and tannery. Smokehouse and stretching room in the rear. Best earnings from exotic hides: alligator, snake, swamp critters. Sidelines in small leather goods.

locations.name: Sundries
locations.description: Dried herbs, pipeweed, exotic fungi, snake-oil medicines. Buys curiosities from adventurers. Sign in Common and Gnomish. Under-the-counter trade in narcotics, poisons, thieves' tools, and magical components is the worst-kept secret in Nebruz.

locations.name: Dorota's Cottage
locations.description: Small house on the village outskirts — seemingly isolated, but Dorota is deeply respected. Village healer and wise woman since the temple closed. Friend of Leeandra.

locations.name: Shuttered Temple
locations.description: A converted storehouse closed by Marsh-Hand decree after an itinerant cleric's aggressive proselytizing alienated the village. Furnishings in warehouse storage. The cleric returns every two weeks to hold private services for his small congregation.

npc.name: Sermoner Peryl
npc.role: Itinerant cleric; travels the river by small boat

locations.name: Arms & Iron
locations.description: The only stone building in Nebruz. Produces ironwork, weapons, arrowheads, and ring and chainmail from bog iron. Work is excellent; final products suffer from low-grade ore. Proprietor is the most taciturn resident of an already taciturn village.

locations.name: Marsh-Hand's Residence
locations.description: Two-story building serving as both village hall and Phaid's home. Ground floor for civic duties; upper floor private chambers. 75% chance Phaid is present during daylight. Rarely available after dark.

locations.name: Kalo the Mad's Home
locations.description: Poorly maintained single-story house. Kalo lost both legs to renegade lizard-folk who tortured and partially ate him; he was rescued by Leeandra. He has never recovered mentally. He begs scraps from the inn, fish house, and private homes, and will not leave after dark — he is convinced his severed legs return each night to collect the rest of him.

dm_notes: Effemi Nuha's class_level (MU9) is a concealed identity — she presents as a simple smith. Dorota knows the truth; Effemi does not know Dorota knows. Wilem Stonenose is hiding from a former thieves' guild and will not respond to Thieves' Cant. Sermoner Peryl's small congregation is unnamed. The renegade lizard-folk band that mutilated Kalo is distinct from the treaty tribes to the northwest and is still at large in the swamp.

session_history.notes: Party overnighted in Nebruz en route from Caelrun to Leeandra's hut. No encounters detailed.
